name: Centro BTT Valderejo Añana Ruta 3 – circular desde Camping Angosto
region_intro: Mountain biking around Valdegovía offers a diverse landscape characterized by river valleys, rolling hills, and forested areas, particularly within the Valderejo Añana Natural Park. The region features a mix of well-maintained trails and more rugged paths, providing varied terrain for mountain bikers. Elevations range from gentle ascents to more challenging climbs towards areas like Sierra Arcena. This makes Valdegovía suitable for different skill levels, from leisurely rides along waterways to more demanding mountain bike trails. Best mountain bike trails around Valdegovía The most popular mountain bike trail is Centro BTT Valderejo Añana Ruta 3 – circular desde Camping Angosto, a 7.8 miles (12.6 km) trail that takes 1 hour 3 minutes to complete, offering a moderate circular route through the natural park. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Centro BTT Valderejo Añana Ruta 2 – circular desde Santuario de Nuestra Señora de Angosto, an easy 3.5 miles (5.6 km) path, ideal for a shorter, accessible ride around the sanctuary. Local mountain bikers also love the Ascent to Sierra Arcena – Ascent to the Sierra de Arcena loop from Villanañe, an 18.6 miles (30.0 km) trail leading through varied terrain with significant elevation gain, often completed in about 2 hours 59 minutes. Mountain biking around Valdegovía is defined by river valleys, forested hills, and the diverse landscapes of Valderejo Añana Natural Park. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy family friendly mountain bike trails to advanced mountain bike routes Valdegovía. The routes in Valdegovía are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 30 reviews. More than 300 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Valdegovía's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available in Valdegovía?
A: There are 17 mountain bike trails in Valdegovía, offering a diverse range of options for different skill levels and preferences.

Q: Are there any easy or family-friendly mountain bike trails in Valdegovía?
A: Yes, Valdegovía features 2 easy mountain bike trails, perfect for families or beginners. A great option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1067531327/centro-btt-valderejo-anana-ruta-2-circular-desde-santuario-de-nuestra-senora-de-angosto" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Centro BTT Valderejo Añana Ruta 2 – circular desde Santuario de Nuestra Señora de Angosto&lt;/a&gt;, an accessible 3.5-mile (5.6 km) path.

Q: Where can I find more challenging mountain bike routes in Valdegovía?
A: For advanced riders, Valdegovía offers 5 difficult mountain bike routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38179428" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route 10 MTB Angosto loop from Villanueva de Valdegovía&lt;/a&gt; is a demanding 35.5-mile (57.2 km) trail with substantial elevation gain, ideal for experienced mountain bikers.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike trails in Valdegovía?
A: Yes, many trails in Valdegovía are circular. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1067489993/centro-btt-valderejo-anana-ruta-3-circular-desde-camping-angosto" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Centro BTT Valderejo Añana Ruta 3 – circular desde Camping Angosto&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 7.8-mile (12.6 km) loop through the natural park.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Valdegovía?
A: The spring and autumn months generally offer the most pleasant conditions for mountain biking in Valdegovía, with milder temperatures and less extreme weather. Summer can be warm, while winter may bring colder temperatures and potential for snow at higher elevations.

Q: What kind of viewpoints can I expect along the mountain bike trails in Valdegovía?
A: Valdegovía's trails often lead to scenic viewpoints, particularly in areas like Sierra Arcena. You can find stunning vistas such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3079407" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views of Pico del Fraile&lt;/a&gt;, offering panoramic views of the surrounding landscape.

Q: Are there any natural attractions or landmarks to see while mountain biking in Valdegovía?
A: Yes, the region is rich in natural beauty. You can explore features like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3079476" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Purón River Gorge&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3374993" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Nervión River&lt;/a&gt;, and the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2824175" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Nervión Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;. The trails also pass by various caves, including the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5594218" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rock Caves of Villanueva de Valdegovía&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about mountain biking in Valdegovía?
A: The mountain bike trails in Valdegovía are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 30 reviews. Riders often praise the diverse landscapes, from river valleys to forested hills, and the well-maintained paths within the Valderejo Añana Natural Park.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails near historical sites or cultural points of interest?
A: Yes, some routes offer a blend of nature and history. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20508153" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Torre de los Varona – Salinas de Añana loop from Villanañe&lt;/a&gt; takes you past the historic Tower of Varona and the ancient salt flats of Añana, providing a cultural dimension to your ride.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails that offer significant elevation gain for a good workout?
A: Absolutely. For those seeking a challenging climb, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10513607" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ascent to Sierra Arcena – Ascent to the Sierra de Arcena loop from Villanañe&lt;/a&gt; is an excellent choice. This 18.6-mile (30.0 km) trail features substantial elevation gain, making for a rewarding and strenuous ride.
tips: The original village dates back to prehistoric times, to the Iron Age. There are some references to it during the Roman times and the Muslim times. Now it is a small town with less than 200 inhabitants.
This is quite an amazing example of 14th century military architecture. It is completely restored and turned into a museum. Guided tours can be arranged through the Valdegovía Tourist Office.
